hook: On Hantavirus outbreak on MV Hondius, only `asia_pacific_regional` used: "expos", "china", "mrna". That changes the story.

tweets.text: 37 buckets, 66 articles, 7 distinct frames in today's coverage.
Frame: US_QUARANTINE_EXCEPTIONALISM (3 buckets). "The US decision not to quarantine passengers of the hantavirus-stricken cruise ship, the MV Hondius, could be dangerous, the chief of the W…" — RT

Frame: SCIENTIFIC_PRESTIGE_CLAIM (2 buckets). "South African medical scientists have, just like with Covid-19, once again done the country proud by working fast and efficiently to discov…" — AllAfrica / Daily Maverick

Frame: ARGENTINA_CULPABILITY_DISPUTE (2 buckets). "Tierra del Fuego epidemiology director Juan Petrina says that the likelihood that the Dutch man linked to the hantavirus outbreak on the MV…" — Buenos Aires Herald

Frame: PANDEMIC_CALIBRATION_DEBATE (2 buckets). "The outbreak of a deadly virus aboard a cruise ship may sound like a familiar story — but while it's a serious scenario, public health figu…" — Axios World

Silence as data: `china` didn't cover this — they covered Hormuz crisis, Trump-Xi summit preparations (CGTN, SCMP covered those angles); the mRNA-gap framing surfaced only in Asia Times, not in Chinese state media itself. instead.
**Asia Times** (asia_pacific_regional): Frames the outbreak as a geopolitical stress-test revealing a US-China mRNA vaccine gap — the only outlet to treat this as a technology-competition story.
